reasoning: The informal statement asks to show that a group of order 351 has a normal Sylow p-subgroup for some prime p dividing its order. The Lean signature asserts ∃ p : ℕ and P : Sylow p G with p.Prime ∧ p ∣ card G ∧ P.Normal, which matches exactly: a prime p dividing |G|, and a normal Sylow p-subgroup P for that prime. In Mathlib, `Sylow p G` is defined as a maximal IsPGroup p subgroup (Sylow.lean line 53) without requiring p to be prime, so the explicit `p.Prime` hypothesis correctly captures the informal "prime p". The `p ∣ card G` condition matches "dividing its order" and is needed to rule out the trivial case where p doesn't divide |G| (in which case ⊥ would be a trivially normal Sylow p-subgroup). The `P.Normal` assertion is the standard subgroup-normality inherited through the coercion. The quantifier structure (∃ p, ∃ P, ...) correctly matches the informal "for some prime p" phrasing, and `Fintype G` with `card G = 351` correctly encodes "group of order 351".
